race.name: Foulée Blanche
race.tagline: Forty-two freestyle kilometers across the Vercors plateau — France's largest Nordic race through alpine meadows and limestone canyons.

vitals.aid_stations: Multiple ravitaillements with warm drinks and local specialties

climate.description: The Vercors plateau at 1,050m elevation delivers cold, snowy winters sheltered by surrounding limestone cliffs. January temperatures range from -10°C to 3°C. Snow coverage is generally reliable thanks to altitude and north-facing aspects, though the southern Alpine location can bring occasional warm spells.
challenges: Vercors wind exposure on open plateau sections
Occasional warm Mediterranean influence can soften snow
Altitude variation creates different snow conditions across the course
January date means short daylight hours

course.primary: Freestyle course traversing the Vercors plateau around Autrans. The route crosses high alpine meadows, forest tracks, and limestone terrain typical of the Vercors massif. Rolling terrain with moderate climbing and panoramic views of the surrounding Alps.
course.surface: Machine-groomed plateau trails through alpine meadow and forest
course.grooming: Professional French federation standard — well-groomed and maintained
features: Vercors plateau — a limestone massif with dramatic cliff scenery
Autrans hosted the 1968 Grenoble Olympics Nordic events
Views of Mont Blanc and the Chartreuse range on clear days
French ravitaillement culture — aid station food is exceptional
Part of the French long-distance Nordic calendar

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: France's flagship Nordic marathon with stunning Vercors scenery and strong field size. Olympic heritage (1968 Grenoble) adds prestige. The French ravitaillement tradition makes aid stations memorable. Snow reliability is the weak point in this southern Alpine location.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: TIER 2 — France's premier citizen ski marathon with Olympic heritage

history.summary: Founded in 1979, La Foulée Blanche is France's largest and most prestigious cross-country ski marathon. The race is held in Autrans on the Vercors plateau, which hosted the Nordic skiing events of the 1968 Grenoble Winter Olympics. The event has grown to attract approximately 2,000 participants, making it the premier event on the French citizen ski racing calendar. The Vercors — a dramatic limestone massif — provides a uniquely scenic setting among European marathon ski races.
notable_facts: Autrans hosted Nordic events at the 1968 Grenoble Winter Olympics
The Vercors massif is a UNESCO Global Geopark
WWII Resistance history — the Vercors was a major maquis stronghold
The name 'Foulée Blanche' translates to 'White Stride'
French aid station culture means exceptional food at ravitaillements
